audit_type: Round 3 hidden-bug hunt (toJSON / array-meta / forensicDump / getter)

rounds.evidence: Object with own toJSON method intercepts JSON.stringify and substitutes serialised output. `evil={__nasty:'payload', toJSON(){return{x:1}}}` hashed identically to clean `{x:1}` — adversary hides nasty payload from forensic checksum.
rounds.fix: prepareForJson skips 'toJSON' key when copying own enumerable keys. Prepared null-proto output has no toJSON, so JSON.stringify cannot invoke it.

rounds.evidence: Arrays in JS can carry non-index own properties (e.g. `arr.metadata='hidden'`). JSON.stringify drops them. Adversary attaches extra payload to an array to bypass checksum.
rounds.fix: prepareForJson detects extra non-index keys on arrays via Object.keys(val).filter(/^\d+$/). Wraps result in {[CANON_TAG_ARRAY_WITH_META]: {items, meta}}.

rounds.angle: forensicDump returns live mutable frame reference
rounds.evidence: dump.frame === stream.frames[i] reference. Caller mutates dump.frame.turn=999 → stream.frames[i].turn=999 — forensic dump rewrote replay state.
rounds.fix: forensicDump now structuredClones frame + events_in_turn + events_prev_turn AND deepFreezes each. Outer dump is Object.freeze()'d.

rounds.angle: Getter throws during canonicalize → R68 service DoS
rounds.evidence: canonicalize({get x(){throw}}) propagates the exception. Adversarial payload (or buggy domain class with throwing getter) crashes R68 forensic pipeline.
rounds.fix: prepareForJson wraps property access in try/catch; on throw, emits CANON_SENTINEL_GETTER_THREW sentinel.

rounds.evidence: Uint8Array([1,2,3,4]) serialised as {"0":1,"1":2,"2":3,"3":4} via own indexed props. Distinct from empty TypedArray. Acceptable encoding for current use.
rounds.result: VERIFIED acceptable (could be tagged in future for stronger forensic — not load-bearing now)

rounds.evidence: [1,,3] → '[1,null,3]'; [1,undefined,3] → '[1,"__SVTK_undefined__",3]'. Distinct canonicals — actually correct: hole and explicit undefined ARE different JS states.

rounds.angle: NUL byte / newline injection in encounter_id
rounds.evidence: encounter_id='evil\x00injected' passes through verbatim. frameId with embedded newline produces distinct hash. Injection chars don't break serialisation.
step(Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER, 'end') produces 20-digit ns value. Well within BigInt range (BigInt has no upper bound).

rounds.angle: Empty array vs empty object canonical

rounds.evidence: Promise serialised as {} (no enumerable own keys). Function → null. Class method (non-enumerable) dropped. Acceptable since these types shouldn't be in serialised state anyway.

files_modified_round3: cmd-engine/output/replay/state_checksum.ts (toJSON skip, array meta tag, getter try/catch, forensicDump deep-clone/freeze)
